commands.review_summary:
The PR changes package-update restart verification to require managed gateway service supervision after a package refresh and adds regression coverage for a missing-supervisor scenario.

PR surface: Source +88, Tests +74, Other +204. Total +366 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible with high confidence: current main can set the post-refresh already-healthy shortcut from gateway health alone, while the linked issue provides concrete WSL2/systemd logs. I did not run a live package update in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for compatibility-sensitive fail-closed updater behavior and missing external real-environment proof; there is no narrow automated code repair from this review.

target.title: fix(update): require service supervision after package refresh
target.body:
Fixes #90930

## Summary

This PR hardens the updater path after a package refresh so a surviving gateway process is not treated as sufficient proof that the managed service is still supervised.

The bug in #90930 is the health-only shortcut after `openclaw update`: if the gateway process remains alive, the updater can skip reinstalling/revalidating the managed service even when the package refresh removed the service unit / launch entry. That leaves the gateway alive only until the next crash, reboot, or restart, after which OpenClaw is no longer supervised.

## What changed

- Add a focused regression test that reproduces the post-refresh missing-supervisor case.
- Split process liveness from managed-service supervision proof.
- After a package refresh, require explicit supervisor validation/reinstall before accepting the update as complete.
- Preserve the existing quick path only for cases where the managed service remains provably installed and active.

## Evidence before the fix

Canonical issue evidence is in #90930, including the full packet in:

- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90930#issuecomment-4639636487

A test-only branch reproduced the failure before the fix:

- `test(update): reproduce missing supervisor after update`
- failing GitHub Actions run: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27064784784

That run demonstrates the current main behavior: a healthy gateway process can satisfy the post-refresh check even when the managed service proof is missing.

## Evidence after the fix

Prepared fix branch:

- `snowzlm:fix/90930-update-systemd-supervision`
- head: `abe9a1dfabc01214ef66b591d85c54f2c7196ce6`

Validation performed on the focused patch scope:

- focused updater regression test passed locally before PR submission
- `oxfmt` passed on the touched updater files before PR submission
- `oxlint` passed on the touched updater files before PR submission
- GitHub Actions run for the fix branch: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27066618320

Note: the full fork CI run had one unrelated job failure outside the updater path (`checks-node-agentic-gateway-methods`); all updater-relevant and lint/type/check jobs reported by that run were green. I reran the failed job before opening this PR so upstream checks can refresh on the submitted PR head.

## Risk

Low-to-medium. The change is intentionally fail-closed after package refresh: if service supervision cannot be proven, the updater repairs/revalidates the managed service instead of trusting only an already-running gateway process.

The tradeoff is an extra managed-service check in the update completion path, but it prevents the worse state where the process is temporarily alive while the boot/restart supervisor is gone.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous health-only shortcut behavior after package refresh.
